meaning: v.仲裁,调停,作为引起...的媒介,居中调停
examples: The Secretary-General was asked to mediate in the dispute.
An independent body was brought in to mediate between staff and management.
Schools are not just a microcosm of society; they mediate it too.
In 2001, Bush appointed Danforth as his envoy to help mediate between warring factions in Sudan.
On Tuesday, Argentine Foreign Minister Hector Timerman asked the United Nations to mediate in the dispute.
